sections.content:
The red block at (31, 16) did not move. I need to find out why.
Looking at the `get_block_info` output from the previous turn:
- Red block (id 14): center [31, 14], boundary [[30, 12], [30, 17], [33, 17], [33, 12]]
- Red block (id 15): center [31, 19], boundary [[30, 18], [30, 21], [33, 21], [33, 18]]
Wait, there are two red blocks? No, one is 'R' (id 14) and one is 'r' (id 15). They are adjacent.
Let's check the colors: 'R' is red, 'r' is dark red.
The target shape on the right is red.
Let's try to click the red block again, but this time I'll try to move it LEFT first to see if it moves at all.
Also, I'll check the blue block.
Blue block (id 9): center [21, 19], boundary [[20, 18], [20, 21], [23, 21], [23, 18]]
Wait, there are many blue blocks (id 18, 20, 23, 25, 26, 28, 29, 31). These are the checkered ones.
The main blue block is id 9.
Let's try clicking the red block (id 14, center [31, 14]) and moving it LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking the red block (id 14) and moving it LEFT.
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 14},
    'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
